titleInfo.title: Beiqi da shamen An Daoyi kejing shiji
北齊大沙門安道壹刻經事迹
Traces of Sutras Engraved by the Great Renunciant An Daoyi during the Northern Qi Dynasty

mods.abstract: In this article, Kiriya further develops his ideas from Kiriya1996, Kiriya2001a and Kiriya2001b. He amends Kiriya1996 by saying that the carvings on Northern and Southern Mount Xiangtang were also calligraphic works by An Daoyi. p. 50-55 gives a table with all inscription sites and their possible relation to An Daoyi. In almost every place, Kiriya finds traces of An Daoyi. Among the Shandong sites, he only excludes Mount gang, and questions Mount yang, because we have no idea what the calligraphy of the carved passage looked like. In Hebei, he accepts one of two "Buddha King of Great Emptiness" carvings (the one dated 563) and the two sutra passages in cave 2 as well as the sixteen Buddhanames at Mount Southern Xiangtang; the Tang Yong stele, the excerpt from the Sutra of Immeasurable Meanings, “Chapter on Virtuose Behavior, First Part” 無量義經，德行品第一 (T#276), and the Three Buddha names taken from the 現在賢劫千佛名經 (T#447：彌勒佛、獅子佛、明炎佛) carved at Northern Mount Xiangtang, and he further does not really exclude the sutras donated by Tang Yong; he doubts Shuiyu Si 水浴寺 (Little Mount Xiangtang, dated 574). In Henan, he does not find traces of An Daoyi. On this basis, Kiriya constructs a hypothetical biography of An Daoyi. First he groups all sites in seven phases: 1 Mount Hongding; 2 Mount Sili, Mount Yin, Mount Yuncui, Mount Shuyuan, Mount Dazhia, Northern Xiangtangshan (Buddhaname); 3 Mount Southern Xiangtang (563-565), Mount Northern Xiangtang, Mount Zhonghuang; 4 Mount Culai, Mount Shuiniu, Mount Yi, Mount Tao; 5 doubtful sites in Henan (林縣洪谷寺，衛輝縣香泉寺) and Hebei (武安縣水浴寺); 6 Mount Tai; 7 Mount Tie, Mount Ge, (Mount Gang). For each site, he discusses An Daoyi’s involvement, thus creating his biography. According to Kiriya, An Daoyi met Tang Yong(phase 3).
